partners.name: Correspondent Bank SWIFT Desk
partners.description: Bank-led support for inward export receipts where audit trail and documentary review matter.
partners.costProfile: Higher-touch bank charges and FX spread, with stronger manual review.
partners.speedProfile: Typically slower because settlement can depend on correspondent banks and document checks.
partners.regulatoryFit: Useful when the finance team wants bank-reviewed export proceeds records and clear purpose-code handling.
partners.bestUseCase: SaaS and services export receipts that need a conservative bank audit trail.
partners.whyThisPartner: Fits this route because the scenario uses bank SWIFT inward remittance for export proceeds, where documentary review and bank records are more important than the lightest workflow.

partners.name: FlowBridge Export Collections
partners.description: Fintech-assisted collection support for repeat export receipts from overseas customers.
partners.costProfile: Lower operating friction than bank-only SWIFT, with platform and FX costs still reviewed as a range.
partners.speedProfile: Good fit for repeat payer patterns after customer, invoice, and collection details are set up.
partners.regulatoryFit: Works best when platform records can be reconciled back to invoices, purpose codes, and export reporting evidence.
partners.bestUseCase: Recurring SaaS or services export receipts with predictable customers and invoice patterns.
partners.whyThisPartner: Fits this route because the scenario involves repeat export collections where local account or fintech-assisted collection records can reduce operational work while preserving traceability.

partners.name: LedgerRail Vendor Payouts
partners.description: Fintech-assisted outward payment support for repeat vendor and service payments.
partners.costProfile: Designed for repeatable vendor payment ranges where platform and FX costs can be compared against bank SWIFT.
partners.speedProfile: Strong fit when vendor details and compliance context can be reused across payments.
partners.regulatoryFit: Appropriate when finance can retain platform confirmations with invoice, agreement, and purpose-code records.
partners.bestUseCase: Recurring vendor payments to UAE, Singapore, UK, or EU counterparties.
partners.whyThisPartner: Fits this route because the scenario is a repeat outward payment use case where fintech-assisted validation and local payout eligibility can reduce operating drag.

partners.name: Anchor Trade Bank Review
partners.description: Bank-supported review for outward remittances tied to trade, shipment, or formal vendor documentation.
partners.costProfile: More documentation-heavy bank pricing profile, useful when formal review matters more than minimum friction.
partners.speedProfile: Usually slower because bank review can depend on invoice, shipment, beneficiary, and purpose details.
partners.regulatoryFit: Strong fit for documented import or trade payments that need a bank-reviewed paper trail.
partners.bestUseCase: Vendor or import/export payments with shipment evidence, purchase orders, or formal bank review needs.
partners.whyThisPartner: Fits this route because the scenario uses a bank-led outward remittance path where trade documentation and beneficiary review are part of the operating requirement.
